recipe_data.title: Tiramisu Panna Cotta Cups
recipe_data.description: A reliable muffin-tin build for Tiramisu Panna Cotta Cups, designed for crispy edges and a tender center.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease a standard 12-cup muffin tin well.
Whisk eggs, milk, melted butter, salt, and pepper until fully combined.
Whisk in flour just until smooth, then fold in cheddar and green onion.
Divide batter evenly among muffin cups, filling each about 3/4 full.
Bake 20-24 minutes, until puffed, lightly golden, and just set in the center.
Rest 5 minutes in pan, then loosen with a thin knife and serve warm.
recipe_data.chef_notes: Use a metal pan for better browning. Let the cups rest before removing so they hold shape.
margaret_notes: At least we're doing the technique correctly.
Check oven temp with a thermometer. Don't trust the dial.
Use a metal pan for better browning. Let the cups rest before removing so they hold shape.

dialogue.message: Fine. FINE. Let's just get this over with. What’s the plan for these Tiramisu Panna Cotta Cups? I demand precision and no shortcuts.

"If I may offer some context, Margaret, in the tradition of Italian culinary craftsmanship, these Tiramisu Panna Cotta Cups are not merely desserts; they are vessels through which the soul of the café society is served. Each layer holds a secret dialogue between bitterness and sweetness, much like a novel's structure. The challenge lies in balancing the delicate equilibrium of flavors, ensuring that each bite tells a story. This, my dear, requires patience and an appreciation for the complexity of flavors, not shortcuts."

I just want to make sure everyone feels heard and that our approach aligns with the café's standards. What do YOU think? I'm really interested in your perspective.

I am confident that through meticulous attention to detail and a deep understanding of flavors, we shall create something truly exquisite."

copy_text.body: There is a certain poetry in the simple act of cooking. Tiramisu Panna Cotta Cups represents more than mere sustenance—it is a meditation on the intersection of tradition and innovation, of comfort and creativity. The muffin tin, that most humble of vessels, transforms ingredients into something greater than the sum of their parts.
